purpose: Add VK ID as a supported Medusa customer identity provider without creating a parallel auth system.
success_outcome: VK ID start and callback produce a validated Medusa provider identity using state and PKCE while retaining no provider token.
anti_goals: Do not create customer/session or storefront UI in this slice.
Do not store VK access or refresh tokens.
Do not use live VK credentials in automated tests.

success_checks: Provider ID vkid implements Medusa provider interface.
State, S256 PKCE, device_id, exchange, and stable user_id mapping pass provider-double tests.
Tokens and raw provider failures are neither persisted nor logged.
evidence_required: Typecheck, lint, T3 human checkpoint, and recovery note.

stop_conditions: VK API behavior cannot satisfy state and S256 PKCE requirements.
Validated email or stable provider subject cannot be obtained.
Implementation would persist or expose provider tokens.
required_handoff: Report changed_files, commands_run, evidence, scope_compliance, and blockers_or_none.
Run /verify and /red-verify; record HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and ROLLBACK_RECOVERY_NOTE before T3 closure.
